note: STAR topology: one separator (3 vars) shared across `fanout` children -> cliques-per-separator = fanout+1. Tests 'more cliques per separator -> bigger gap'. RESULT (5 seeds median[IQR]): MF R2 == DENSE at every cell. The block deficit JUMPS when a separator is shared (fanout 1 -> >=2) but then PLATEAUS ~0.16-0.28 R2 within seed noise -- it does NOT grow monotonically with fanout. Hypothesis 'more sharing -> monotonically bigger gap' NOT supported (PARTIAL REFUTATION); 'sharing matters' supported. Mechanism: at fixed scarcity the residual vars cap both solvers, and block's per-measurement error on the shared separator is set once fanout>=2; more children add equally-sep-affected measurements, not more per-measurement error. Small-d noise dominates fanout=1 (d=9).
